page: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and INT8 operations.

This server is characterized by a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, providing 512.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 32.0 GB per virtual core. This memory density positions the instance within the memory-optimized category. The server does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external Premium Storage options. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

In benchmark testing, the E64-16s_v6 demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads. Single-threaded gzip compression reached 31.31 GB/sec, and the Geekbench single-core score of 2115.0 is categorized as strong. Large-block memory write bandwidth is also strong at 90.18 GB/sec, while read bandwidth remains average at 132.03 GB/sec. Multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as the stress-ng multi-core CPU test and bzip3 decompression, fall within the average performance tier. For lightweight language model tasks, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 288.15 tokens/sec on a 135M parameter model, though performance scales down to an average of 12.6 tokens/sec for a 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high memory density relative to its compute allocation, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads without the expense of scaling up vCPU counts. However, the lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring additional configuration for external storage volumes. The hardware profile and benchmark results, including a Passmark database score of 10310.5 and Redis SET throughput of 22.37 million operations per second, indicate suitability for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores and provides 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance does not include local storage, relying instead on external Premium Storage. Benchmark results show strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and large-block memory write bandwidth, alongside average multi-core CPU and memory read speeds. With a Redis throughput of 22.37 million operations per second and a Passmark database score of 10310.5, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8573C CPU. It lacks local storage but offers strong memory write and compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v6 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU with no local storage.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized configuration featuring high RAM density, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and strong single-threaded compression performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores at 4.2 GHz
512.0 GB of RAM providing a high 32.0 GB per vCPU ratio
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes
Strong single-threaded gzip compression and large-block memory write performance
Supports Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications
